title: UI11 and UI15 are deprecated
officialSummary: Starting with Australia, the legacy interfaces known as UI11 and UI15 no longer receive enhancements or defect fixes and will no longer be supported.
editorialImplication: Any remaining dependency on legacy navigation or rendering now needs an explicit transition plan rather than passive acceptance.
recommendedActions: Inventory users and processes still relying on UI11 or UI15.
Add Next Experience regression coverage for critical journeys.
Assign an owner and target date for remaining migrations.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Legacy Application Manager is deprecated
officialSummary: Legacy Application Manager is deprecated as of Australia Patch 1, and its bookmarks redirect to the new Application Manager experience.
editorialImplication: Operational documentation and training that point to the legacy experience will become misleading.
recommendedActions: Review bookmarks, runbooks, and internal training for legacy links.
Validate key application-management tasks in the new experience.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Dashboard overview template moves to legacy
officialSummary: The Dashboard overview template is renamed as deprecated and moved under Legacy templates; ServiceNow recommends the Dashboard library template.
editorialImplication: New workspace designs should not deepen dependency on a template already marked for eventual deprecation.
recommendedActions: Stop selecting the legacy template for new work.
Identify high-value dashboards that should move to Dashboard library.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Multisource Report Builder is removed
officialSummary: Australia removes the CMDB Multisource Report Builder and directs users to CMDB 360 in CMDB Workspace or Service Graph Workspace.
editorialImplication: Reports, operational procedures, or training built around the removed builder may fail or become unusable after upgrade.
recommendedActions: Inventory Multisource Report Builder usage.
Recreate required reports in CMDB 360 before production upgrade.
Include report consumers in acceptance testing.

source.title: Removed features and products in Australia

title: CMDB tasks gain granular role access
officialSummary: CMDB tables previously requiring admin or itil_admin are also accessible to sn_cmdb_admin, while tables requiring itil are also accessible to sn_cmdb_editor.
editorialImplication: Teams can reduce broad privileged access while retaining the administration and editing capabilities needed for CMDB work.
recommendedActions: Map existing elevated-role assignments to the granular CMDB roles.
Test least-privilege role bundles in sub-production.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: IRE can generate de-duplication tasks for related tables
officialSummary: IRE can be configured to generate de-duplication tasks for selected lookup or related tables during identification.
editorialImplication: Duplicate remediation can become governed operational work instead of an ad hoc cleanup activity.
recommendedActions: Select the lookup tables where duplicates create material risk.
Pilot task ownership, routing, and closure criteria.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Create CI agentic workflow requires sn_cmdb_admin
officialSummary: The Create configuration item agentic workflow now requires sn_cmdb_admin instead of sn_cmdb_editor.
editorialImplication: Existing AI-assisted CI creation users may lose access unless role design and entitlements are updated deliberately.
recommendedActions: Identify users and AI identities that invoke the workflow.
Review whether administrative access is appropriate before assigning it.
Regression-test the workflow with target personas.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: CMDB Now Assist panel skills require another role
officialSummary: CMDB AI skills running in the Now Assist panel require both cmdb_dedup_admin and now_assist_panel_user.
editorialImplication: Role bundles that worked on Zurich may no longer be sufficient for the same user journey.
recommendedActions: Update the access matrix for CMDB Now Assist personas.
Test positive and negative access cases after upgrade.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Cloud Discovery Workspace is on a deprecation path
officialSummary: Cloud Discovery Workspace is hidden and not activated on new instances while Discovery Admin Workspace is the current experience.
editorialImplication: Teams still operating the legacy workspace should treat Australia as a prompt to complete their transition.
recommendedActions: Confirm whether Cloud Discovery Workspace is active.
Compare required workflows in Discovery Admin Workspace.
Plan user enablement and retirement milestones.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Alert Clustering Definitions are deprecated
officialSummary: Alert Clustering Definitions are deprecated and replaced by Alert Automation in Service Operations Workspace; existing configurations remain supported.
editorialImplication: Supported does not mean strategic: new clustering work should target Alert Automation and legacy definitions need an exit plan.
recommendedActions: Inventory active clustering definitions and owners.
Validate equivalent behavior in Alert Automation.
Stop creating new legacy definitions.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Service Management Dashboard is deprecated
officialSummary: Service Management Dashboard is deprecated, unsupported, and unavailable for new activation.
editorialImplication: Executive and operational reporting that depends on this dashboard needs a replacement decision before the dependency becomes urgent.
recommendedActions: Identify dashboard audiences and recurring decisions.
Map required indicators to a supported analytics experience.
Retire duplicate or unused reports during migration.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Incident assist skill is archived
officialSummary: Starting with Australia Patch 2, Incident assist is deprecated, moved to the Archived folder, and no longer available for use.
editorialImplication: Any production workflow or value case built around this skill must be checked against a supported replacement.
recommendedActions: Confirm whether Incident assist is enabled or referenced.
Identify the supported replacement for each use case.
Update operational guidance and adoption reporting.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Suggested steps is being replaced by LEAP
officialSummary: From Australia Patch 3, Suggested steps is being prepared for future deprecation and replaced by Learning Enhanced Automation Platform (LEAP), which requires the sn_itom_leap plugin.
editorialImplication: Migration includes both product behavior and activation prerequisites, so it belongs on the roadmap rather than only in upgrade testing.
recommendedActions: Identify Suggested steps adoption and success measures.
Assess LEAP licensing and plugin prerequisites.
Run a controlled use-case comparison before migration.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: SOW exposes reference auto-load configuration
officialSummary: SOW Admin Center can configure automatic reference-search behavior for fields such as Configuration item, Service offering, and Service.
editorialImplication: A platform-level setting can improve agent speed, but large reference sets may require performance-conscious testing.
recommendedActions: Test the setting with realistic data volumes.
Measure agent effort and reference-search latency before enabling broadly.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: SOW expands PIR collaboration actions
officialSummary: Eligible PIR co-contributors can update state, publish, refresh incident summary data, create custom events, and manage co-contributors.
editorialImplication: Post-incident review work can stay in the operational workspace with broader, explicit collaboration roles.
recommendedActions: Review PIR contributor policy and role assignments.
Pilot the expanded workflow with the incident-management team.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Catalog Builder moves UI policy management
officialSummary: Catalog Builder removes the UI policies icon from question editing; users now manage policies through the UI Policy tab.
editorialImplication: The underlying capability remains, but author training and procedural screenshots can become outdated immediately.
recommendedActions: Update catalog-author job aids and walkthroughs.
Include the revised authoring path in upgrade communications.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Build Agent becomes the default in Studio
officialSummary: Build Agent is the default for AI-assisted app generation in ServiceNow Studio, while users can change the preference.
editorialImplication: AI-assisted development becomes a default path, increasing the importance of coding standards, review controls, and entitlement clarity.
recommendedActions: Define where Build Agent may be used and what review is mandatory.
Brief developers on the default and preference controls.
Add generated changes to existing peer-review and test gates.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: ServiceNow Studio is available by default
officialSummary: ServiceNow Studio no longer needs to be downloaded from the ServiceNow Store and is available on the platform by default.
editorialImplication: Environment preparation and developer onboarding can be simplified, while governance must assume the tool is present.
recommendedActions: Remove obsolete Store-install steps from environment runbooks.
Validate roles and developer access in each environment.

source.title: Upgrade information for all Australia features and products

title: Studio adds deployment-request visibility
officialSummary: Australia Patch 3 lets developers view deployment requests in Studio and attach update sets to group related changes.
editorialImplication: Teams can connect development context to controlled deployment work without switching tools as often.
recommendedActions: Compare the capability with the existing deployment process.
Pilot it on a low-risk application before changing team standards.

source.title: New features and products in Australia

title: Flow API adds Service Catalog support
officialSummary: ServiceNow SDK 4.4 adds Flow API support for Service Catalog triggers and actions.
editorialImplication: Full-stack application teams gain a supported path for catalog-related automation in SDK-managed development.
recommendedActions: Identify custom catalog automation that could use the supported API.
Verify SDK version and packaging compatibility before adoption.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Next Experience Inspector adds event and state diagnostics
officialSummary: NED Tools add event exclusion filters and a journal of state updates, property updates, dispatched actions, and handled actions.
editorialImplication: Workspace debugging can become more systematic, especially for noisy event flows and state-driven components.
recommendedActions: Add the new Inspector capabilities to workspace debugging guidance.
Use them to document one recurring UI diagnostic scenario.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Upgrade Console coordinates compatible app versions
officialSummary: Upgrade Console can update related Now Assist applications to compatible versions when an application in the compatibility matrix is upgraded.
editorialImplication: Coordinated upgrades can reduce version drift, but teams still need to understand the applications included in the change set.
recommendedActions: Review the compatibility matrix before scheduling application upgrades.
Include dependent applications in scope, communications, and regression tests.

source.title: New features and products in Australia

title: Upgrade Console adds ATF troubleshooting assistance
officialSummary: The ATF troubleshooting agent is available from the post-upgrade testing activity in a non-production instance.
editorialImplication: Failed regression tests can be triaged closer to the upgrade workflow, potentially shortening diagnosis time.
recommendedActions: Trial the agent against known ATF failures in sub-production.
Keep human review and evidence requirements in the defect workflow.

source.title: New features and products in Australia

title: Security-sensitive properties receive secure defaults
officialSummary: Glide properties that can affect instance security are set to secure values by default in Australia.
editorialImplication: A safer baseline may also expose custom behavior that relied on a less restrictive property value.
recommendedActions: Review the Glide Property Hardening maintenance information.
Compare affected property values in a clone before upgrade.
Add impacted integrations and public experiences to regression scope.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Most platform plugins load Query ACLs automatically
officialSummary: Most platform plugins load preconfigured Query ACLs during installation; existing custom Query ACLs are preserved but loaded as inactive.
editorialImplication: Access behavior and previously active custom controls deserve explicit verification after plugin installation or upgrade.
recommendedActions: Inventory custom Query ACLs before upgrade.
Review inactive custom rules and preconfigured replacements.
Test high-risk query access with representative roles.

source.title: Changes to Australia features and products

title: Demand Management gains a Next Experience workspace
officialSummary: Next Experience for Demand Management consolidates demand creation, assessment, collaboration, and conversion with guided tabs, Playbooks, and Docs integration.
editorialImplication: This is a candidate operating-model change, not simply a visual refresh, because governance stages and collaboration can move into one experience.
recommendedActions: Map the current demand lifecycle and exceptions.
Prototype the guided stages with product and portfolio stakeholders.
Define coexistence or migration from classic experiences.

source.title: New features and products in Australia

title: Legacy PPM Scenario Planning is being deprecated
officialSummary: The Scenario Planning for PPM plugin is hidden and unavailable for new activation; alternatives are Strategic Planning Workspace or Portfolio Planning Workspace.
editorialImplication: Portfolio-planning processes should migrate to the workspace aligned with the organization’s SPM license and target operating model.
recommendedActions: Confirm the active plugin and license tier.
Map scenario-planning use cases to the supported workspace.
Plan data, process, and user migration.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Project Workbench is no longer supported
officialSummary: Starting with Australia, Project Workbench is no longer deployed, enhanced, supported, or available for activation.
editorialImplication: Active use is a direct upgrade-readiness risk that needs a replacement and adoption plan.
recommendedActions: Measure current Project Workbench usage.
Select and validate the supported target experience.
Complete training and cutover before production upgrade.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: PMO dashboard is being deprecated
officialSummary: The PMO dashboard is hidden and unavailable for new activation; Execution dashboard is the supported alternative.
editorialImplication: Portfolio reporting consumers need validation that the replacement preserves their decisions, not only their charts.
recommendedActions: Inventory PMO dashboard audiences and measures.
Reconcile metric definitions with Execution dashboard.
Plan communication and retirement.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products

title: Investment Portal is being deprecated
officialSummary: Investment Portal is hidden and unavailable for new activation; Portfolio Planning Workspace provides the alternative capability.
editorialImplication: Investment-planning journeys, links, and stakeholder training should move to Portfolio Planning Workspace.
recommendedActions: Document active Investment Portal journeys and integrations.
Validate them in Portfolio Planning Workspace.
Update navigation, links, and training.

source.title: Deprecation information for all Australia features and products
